entry.content: test post

entry.title: test post
entry.content: I am here. Thanks, Shanna, for setting this up!

entry.title: Copies of <u>On Religion</u>
entry.content: Please contact Dan if you need a copy of the book. He should have five copies sometime next week. We can order more if the demand continues to grow!

entry.title: signing on
entry.content: Melissa here. Still no book. Barnes and Noble didn't have it in their big store, but I'm still looking for it locally. I figured if I ordered it online, it wouldn't get here in time (seeing as I'm still waiting for books I ordered from one store in November). If I can get a copy from someone, I'd appreciate it. I won't draw dirty pictures in it or anything, except on request. I'll contact Dan though.

entry.title: Joshua signing in ...
entry.content: Yeah, thanks for setting this up. i've ordered a copy of <u>On Religion</u> from Amazon. It should be in next week.

entry.title: Admin Questions?
entry.content:
• Can one of the admins implement a solution for the name dupes?

  • Can the said solution be extensible as to accommodate the possibility of future dupes?

  • How are we going to facilitate comments to posts?


Thank you in advance for your consideration on these matters...

entry.title: Classification of Future
entry.content: Caputo applies a binary dichotomy to the abstract concept,future: Present Future, and Absolute Future. This is the opening of his discussion on God being associated with the Impossible (page 7 - Kauser should still be OK!). I post this here as my first example of why I love the style Caputo employs in this work. This style of which I speak is one that is assertive and accessible. He presents arguments in the binary (oh no, not only two choices!!!) and successfully avoids the temptation to drive a stake in the ground to mark the location for the cornerstone of the foundation for some massive vertical structure (read Augustine, Kant, et al.).

entry.title: "What makes me feel God's pleasure?"
entry.content:
I came across this question **via the blogless Phillip** in a Christianity Today article entitled: A Believer's To-Be List. I thought it interestingly similar to Caputo's question, "What do you love when you love your God?". This question was the fodder for much of our conversation this past Monday (12/08). The article to which I link is a list of interesting points. The body of the point that I highlight in the Title of this post:

"Find what Eric Liddell found: something that allows you to feel God's pleasure. When the sprinter's sister worried that his participation in the Olympics might derail his missionary career, Eric responded, "God made me fast. And when I run, I feel his pleasure." What makes me feel God's pleasure? I must identify it, and then run."

entry.title: Testing Shout Out Code
entry.content: This is a test of the new "Shout Out" capability.

entry.title: The question
entry.content:
"What do you love when you love your God?"

The beauty of this question is that it doesn't presuppose what God loves. Instead it places the question soley on the believer and her/his perspective.

I simplfy the question as: "what are we really loving?" Are we in love with the God-who-is-there (commonly characterized as: omniscient, omnipresent, wholly other) or are we actually in love with loving the concept of God? Can we label it "wrong" (or "not sufficient") to love loving God? Can we love God if God is wholly other? Am I priviledged to even use the word "we"?

I've been reading Soulwind by Scott Morse-- a wonderful modern parable/faerie tale/myth. I love the moment between the turtle and the boy--

TURTLE: You're way ahead of yourself.
BOY: I am the way.
("He would've walked back home, but that would've been redundant. He was home.")

entry.title: The Holy Observer: Saddam Hussein Converts to Christianity in Custody
entry.content:
Saddam Hussein Converts to Christianity in Custody
Southern Baptists call for immediate release of former dictator

entry.title: fruitless endeavors?
entry.content: while others contend that they simply are having trouble obtaining the text, i will boldly admit that i have made no aggressive attempts to obtain the book for which the substance of our current threads are concerning. i prefer to recieve my knowledge second hand as to cloak my illiterate nature. also i find the distorted version of most texts more exciting than the original well versed versions. besides, how relevant can original text be anyway?

entry.title: tonight?
entry.content:
hello friends. trust you have all had a merry december. with the holidays still in play it seems prudent to defer any formal post-post time until after the new year.

i will be working tonight, but would welcome any guests who want to hang out later in the evening. fyi, my cell phone has changed. should you not have the new number (which no one does actually) please email me and i will send it to you.

peace to you and your coming year.

d

entry.title: images
entry.content: i'm working on upgrading to blogger pro so we can post images; in the meantime check out some monday night splendor over at sdt

entry.title: There was a knock at my door ...
entry.content: On Religion finally arrived at my doorstep (along with One Nation Under Goods, a book about mall tactics in America and how it has affected our society). i look forward to actually showing up at the discussion group this Monday. That is, if we are meeting.

entry.title: Meet Up?
entry.content:
Monday, Jan 5th, Anytime after ~7pmish. Dan's.
Last time to see Dan before he dares the terror filled skies to Europe.

Agenda:

  • On Religion,
  • Where to meet next week,
  • Civic duty and Social mayhem

Bring:
  • Whatever - All I have is liquor and ice cream

entry.title: Social Mayhem
entry.content: indeed, it seemed to be much more of the mayhem than the duty, but i enjoyed it all the more. what with bunny ears and pistachio missiles, it was a good night. have fun in london. drink tea for me.

entry.title: Monday Night
entry.content:
Everyone is welcome to come to my place any time after seven pm.


~Shanna

God is a question, not an answer. (p. 117)

entry.title: Tales From The Derridean Nights
entry.content: While not the likes of Inklings, post-post (we need a better way to refer to this) is pouring the ale and setting out to consider more of the primary source behind Caputo's, On Religion in the acclaimed film Derrida this Monday night at 7pm @ Dan's place.

entry.title: there is a deeply religious element within us all, with or without religion....
entry.content:
(p 109)

hello all,

i'd like to invite you to my place this monday, a little after 7pm. i'm no professor, no facilitator of discourse.

but i want to talk about this book.

entry.title: uh, yeah
entry.content: kausar has a question.

entry.title: HELP!!!!
entry.content: i just sent an email and posted on boothead, but i need a roommate and i need one fast. i have until the end of the week to decide if i stay here and renew the lease, or move elsewhere. email to bkeeler@bear.com during the day (work email)

last call for caputo

next monday, feb 23.
feedback on Derrida the movie, On Religion the book and/or other various things.

read. watch. eat chips.

entry.title: diction
entry.content:
this is what i heard Trev read to me a moment ago from some random Google result set:

"diction is the most powerful psychic enemy of humanity's desire for god."

what?

oh, addiction.

diction is a more interesting culprit.

entry.title: considering the impossible
entry.content: a game of Jacks - tonight @ dan's - anytime between 7 - 8:00pm

entry.title: Potentially of interest
entry.content: Trickster

entry.title: In America
entry.content:
A few of us are going to see In America at the Inwood tonight @ 7.35pm. Please come if you are so inclined.

Tickets
More

Desmond Tutu is speaking tonight (Thursday March 25th) at UTA Texas Hall at 7:30pm.

The event is open to the public and free of charge.

entry.title: Geocaching
entry.content: Let's go Geocaching in a couple weeks. Anyone?

entry.title: It looks like this quiz thinks that I am a Neo-Quaker.
entry.content:
Belief-O Matic

1. Liberal Quakers (100%)
2. Neo-Pagan (100%)

entry.title: This Weekend
entry.content:
saturday:

bethany is looking for someone to accompany her to the fry street fair in Deep Ellum. paco's band will be playing at 8pm. tix are $10 if you purchase tonight, $15 tomorrow.

----------------
sunday:

let's brunch at the meridian room -- we can frolic through fair park (spring weather and murals on the sides of exhibit buildings!) afterwards.

entry.title: Legion of Doom Road Trip?
entry.content:
some of us are road tripping to Nashville for this.

we will leave on the 18th and return the 22nd/23rd. we have all registered as members of the Legion of Doom. there is a student discount and a group discount if we get 5+ people. there is still room if anyone is interested. lodging may be an issue, but that should not stop you. Kaus, Justin and I took a road trip to Minnesota in 2002 planning to sleep in the car. we ended up in a suite with a very cool guy from San Antonio. no suite guarantees of course. the unknown is part of the adventure.

entry.title: writing!
entry.content:
there's an interesting thread on one of leah's locked livejournal posts -- here's an excerpt from boudoir_loft's comment:

think we should revisit our idea to morph caputo mondays into a writing group. i think in addition to that we could arrange a sort of electronic writing collective -- a list of people we know who are willing to take and mark the hell out of a manuscript, with the understanding that the favor will be returned accordingly.

any ideas on how this could best be executed? a group blog, or an orkut group, perhaps?

i would love it, especially, if... this could extend to forms of writing like non-fiction (manifestos, magna cartas, critical commentary, etc.), screenplays, comic books, etc.

what do you guys think? you want to meet at my place on monday @ 7 to discuss the possibilities?

entry.title: scarborough faire!
entry.content: we need to go soon. it only runs through may 31. i'll plan if people will tell me what weekend will be best. we could make the trip after brunch one sunday.

entry.title: hey ya'll
entry.content: sorry i missed the trip, i would have liked to have gone but i didn't even know i was on this blog til just now. so perhaps i can make it up by cutting my plaid pants and fashioning a catholic school girl kilt. miss you guys, got most of the fesces off my fan blades. shoot me some respondage when you get a chance... i haven't been to the shamens house since they moved to arlingotn which is crap since i live in arlington as well. i hope i'm not breaking the format here with a bunch of personal drool. just excited to see the familiar words of well missed friends and lovers. i'm going to start an open table multifaith community/bookclub of tainted intellectuals called the legion of doom... who's with me!?

entry.title: Writing group
entry.content:
bring your laptop to my place at 7:30! (pen and paper will do just as well, of course.) we'll have an informal writing time. leah will provide prompts to those who need them.

there will be cookies.

if you have time tonight... come see Polyphonic with us. tomorrow: The Gin Blossoms.

The Polyphonic Spree
Presented by The Dallas Observer
American Airlines Plaza Stage
Virtually overnight this 22 member group, led by former Tripping Daisy front man Tim DeLaughter, have defined a new music genre with their uplifting musical message that's catchy pop minimally laced with a smidgen of lively Godspell-like attitude thrown into the mix. Widely embraced by media and critics alike, the group boasts a ten-member choir, a pair of keyboardists, as well as a percussionist, bassist, guitarist, flautist, trumpeter, trombonist, violist, a French horn player, a theremin player, and an electronic effects wizard. Currently on tour with the legendary David Bowie, "the Spree" got the call of a lifetime recently when they were given the nod to co-headline the Lollapalooza Tour later this summer alongside The String Cheese Incident, The Flaming Lips, Morrissey, and Sonic Youth.

entry.title: speaking of....
entry.content: lollapalooza is coming to the smirnoff center aug 24-25. polyphonic is day 2. we should plan to go. yay!

entry.title: Brunch today
entry.content:
1pm at Gopal (call Shan if you need directions or would like to carpool)
(I'll probably need a ride!)

I need some assistance procuring for this installation:

1. Video camera and tripod. Preferrably some remote control for the camera as well.

2. Two buckets. Like 3 gallon paint buckets. Or anything like that. Flower pots. ETc.

3. Food. We can coordinate this later on.

I'll need an RSVP as well. Why don't you comment as to what dates in mid-late June would work or not work for you.

While we're at it why don't we move to Blogger comments on this sucker?

PS This will not be an art free-for-all; but if you would like to perform, display, or sell any work at the event you are free to co-ordinate with me beforehand. Well beforehand would be preferrable.

PPS Please spread the word. The more the merrier.

PPPS This may offend staunch Catholics.
Tomorrow night, Gary Jules in the tea room.

entry.title: Midnight Polyphonic Party
entry.content:
Together We're Heavy Midnight Sale--Good Records
July 12, 2004
8pm-2am

Good Records
617 N. Good Latimer
Dallas, TX 75204
USA
214-752-4663

"Together We're Heavy" by The Polyphonic Spree

Sodas, Waters, Beer, Margaritas, and Snacks will be provided

1.Sonic Appetizer-Listening To "The Beginning Stages of."
2. Screening of DVD."The Adventure Of Listening"
3. Screening of Exclusive DVD."The Earth Giving Birth"
4. Be the first to hear not just the album but a mind blowing.5.1 Mix of
"Together We're Heavy" presented along with an astounding visual
accompaniment

At the conclusion of the listening/viewing party, The Polyphonic Spree
will be signing "Together We're Heavy"
Rudds are out of town. Katie (housesitter) is working. No movie night tonight.

Help me sell me couches!!

http://dallas.craigslist.org/fur/34964266.html

Please! Negotiable!

entry.title: Movie Premier Thursday Night
entry.content:
Hey Guys-
Wanted to invite you all out to the premiere of my new short film, Youngster. The two leads in the film are Al Thompson, who played Danny Glover's son in The Royal Tenenbaums and played alongside Mandy Moore in A Walk To Remember, and Derek Jamaar Taylor who just finished up his run as the young Simba in The Lion King On Broadway. It's going to be a terrific evening at the Angelika. Feel free to bring some friends along and I hope to see you all out. Best- Will

entry.title: movie?
entry.content: Before Sunset - The Magnolia - 8pm - Tonight (Friday, July 9)

entry.title: 8pm - 2am
entry.content:
The beautiful, daunting, and gracious new album by The Polyphonic
Spree...."Together We're Heavy" will be celebrated tonight at Good
Records.

Food, Drinks, DVD Viewings, CD Signing........

Good Records
617 N. Good Latimer
Dallas, TX
75204
we should watch these at movie night.

Hey, SmartCommons has been birthed. If any of you have not signed up yet come try it out. Search for me and add me as a Friend.

The project is all about sharing--your things, your ideas, your gatherings--with the people you already trust. It's a pretty simple idea...

more

entry.title: anybody out there?
entry.content: hey! no posts in 5 years! crazy... anybody still check this thing?
